title: To provide for the issuance of a Manatee Semipostal Stamp.
plain_summary: This bill tells the US Postal Service to make a special manatee stamp that costs more money than a regular stamp. When people buy these manatee stamps, the extra money will go to the US Fish and Wildlife Service. The Fish and Wildlife Service will use this money to help protect manatees and the places where they live. This is a way to raise money for manatee protection by selling special stamps to people who want to help.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committee on Natural Resources, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for conside

sections.title: Section 1  Manatee Semipostal Stamp
sections.text: The United States Postal Service must create a special stamp called the Manatee Semipostal Stamp. A semipostal stamp is a special postage stamp that costs more than regular postage, with the extra money going to a good cause. The Postal Service must follow all the existing rules and laws that apply to these types of special stamps. All the extra money collected from selling these stamps will go to the United States Fish and Wildlife Service. This agency will use the money to protect manatees and the places where manatees live in the United States. The Postal Service must make these stamps available for people to buy for at least two years. The stamps must start being sold no later than twelve months after this law is passed.
